findings.description: The HTTP response does not include the content-security-policy header.
findings.owasp: A05:2025 Security Misconfiguration / API8:2023 Security Misconfiguration
findings.evidence: Headers seen: cache-control, connection, content-length, content-type, date, etag, vary
findings.impact: Browsers cannot apply the corresponding mitigation if the header is absent.
findings.remediation: Configure the missing header at the reverse proxy or web framework layer.

findings.description: The HTTP response does not include the x-frame-options header.
findings.owasp: A05:2025 Security Misconfiguration / API8:2023 Security Misconfiguration
findings.evidence: Headers seen: cache-control, connection, content-length, content-type, date, etag, vary
findings.impact: Browsers cannot apply the corresponding mitigation if the header is absent.
findings.remediation: Configure the missing header at the reverse proxy or web framework layer.

findings.evidence: Headers seen: cache-control, connection, content-length, content-type, date, etag, vary
findings.impact: Browsers cannot apply the corresponding mitigation if the header is absent.
findings.remediation: Configure the missing header at the reverse proxy or web framework layer.

findings.description: The HTTP response does not include the referrer-policy header.
findings.owasp: A05:2025 Security Misconfiguration / API8:2023 Security Misconfiguration
findings.evidence: Headers seen: cache-control, connection, content-length, content-type, date, etag, vary
findings.impact: Browsers cannot apply the corresponding mitigation if the header is absent.
findings.remediation: Configure the missing header at the reverse proxy or web framework layer.

findings.description: The HTTP response does not include the strict-transport-security header.
findings.owasp: A05:2025 Security Misconfiguration / API8:2023 Security Misconfiguration
findings.evidence: Headers seen: cache-control, connection, content-length, content-type, date, etag, vary
findings.impact: Browsers cannot apply the corresponding mitigation if the header is absent.
findings.remediation: Configure the missing header at the reverse proxy or web framework layer.

findings.type: Public API route catalog exposes endpoint map
findings.description: The server returned a structured JSON route catalog without authentication. Listing admin, checkout, or account endpoints simplifies access-control attacks.
findings.owasp: API9:2023 Improper Inventory Management / A01:2025 Broken Access Control
findings.evidence: top-level JSON keys: service, version, docs, endpoints
reproduction: Send GET http://172.23.112.1:8080/api with no cookies or Authorization header.
Review the JSON for route/endpoint listings.
findings.impact: Attackers map the full API — including sensitive operations — before any blind guessing.
findings.remediation: Remove or authenticate machine-readable API indexes in production.

findings.description: robots.txt is reachable and lists Disallow paths worth reviewing during assessment.
findings.evidence: Disallow: /admin, Disallow: /db.config.bak, Disallow: /api/admin, Disallow: /api/coupons/apply, Disallow: /api/orders/*/receipt, Disallow: /api/account/settings
findings.impact: robots.txt is informational only, but its content can highlight non-public paths.
findings.remediation: Do not place sensitive paths in robots.txt; require authorisation server-side instead.

findings.remediation: Maintain an API inventory and remove or protect unused endpoints.

A malformed parameter caused a verbose or inconsistent error page.
findings.owasp: A05:2025 Security Misconfiguration / API8:2023 Security Misconfiguration
reproduction: Observe verbose error output or inconsistent handling.
findings.impact: Verbose errors can reveal routing, framework, or defensive-control behavior.
findings.remediation: Return generic errors and log detailed diagnostics server-side only.

findings.description: A marker payload was reflected into the HTTP response.
reproduction: Search the response body for the marker payload.
findings.impact: Reflected input may become executable script if output encoding is missing.
findings.remediation: Contextually encode reflected data and validate input.

findings.description: An API endpoint returned structured data without authentication.
findings.impact: Public inventory endpoints expand the discoverable attack surface.
findings.remediation: Document intentionally public APIs and remove stale or hidden endpoints.

findings.description: An API endpoint returned structured data without authentication.
findings.owasp: API3:2023 Broken Object Property Level Authorization / A01:2025 Broken Access Control
findings.evidence: Keys: account, admin, auth, cart, challenges, coupons, docs, endpoints, health, orders, products, service, supportChat, user, users, version
findings.impact: Sensitive object properties can expose credentials, tokens, roles, emails or payment data.
findings.remediation: Filter sensitive fields and enforce object/property authorization.

findings.description: An API endpoint returned structured data without authentication.
findings.evidence: Keys: category_id, category_name, created_at, created_by, description, id, image_url, name, price, products, stock, updated_at
findings.impact: Public inventory endpoints expand the discoverable attack surface.
findings.remediation: Document intentionally public APIs and remove stale or hidden endpoints.

findings.description: An API endpoint returned structured data without authentication.
findings.evidence: Keys: category, challenges, description, difficulty, id, label, learningObjectives, name, panel, route, slug, solved, solvedAt, summary, surface, title
findings.impact: Public inventory endpoints expand the discoverable attack surface.
findings.remediation: Document intentionally public APIs and remove stale or hidden endpoints.

findings.description: An API endpoint returned structured data without authentication.
findings.impact: Public structured APIs should be reviewed and documented as intended exposure.
findings.remediation: Classify public APIs and remove accidental exposure.

findings.description: An API endpoint returned structured data without authentication.
findings.impact: Public inventory endpoints expand the discoverable attack surface.
findings.remediation: Document intentionally public APIs and remove stale or hidden endpoints.

findings.description: 15 endpoint(s) advertise state-changing HTTP methods.
findings.owasp: API5:2023 Broken Function Level Authorization / API6:2023 Unrestricted Access to Sensitive Business Flows
findings.evidence: Allowed methods: GET,HEAD,PUT,PATCH,POST,DELETE; examples: http://172.23.112.1:8080/api/auth/jwt-flag, http://172.23.112.1:8080/api/products/search?q=test, http://172.23.112.1:8080/api, http://172.23.112.1:8080/api/admin, http://172.23.112.1:8080/api/coupons/apply
findings.impact: Unsafe methods can become exploitable if authorization or CSRF controls are weak.
findings.remediation: Expose only required methods and enforce authorization on state changes.

findings.description: Repeated invalid login attempts were accepted without HTTP 429 / lockout responses.
findings.owasp: API4:2023 Unrestricted Resource Consumption / A07:2025 Identification and Authentication Failures
reproduction: Send 8 repeated POSTs with bogus credentials to http://172.23.112.1:8080/api/auth/login
findings.impact: Attackers may automate brute force, scraping, or resource consumption workflows.
findings.remediation: Add rate limits, lockouts, and abuse detection to authentication endpoints.

findings.description: A state-changing endpoint accepted a boundary/invalid value.
findings.owasp: A04:2025 Insecure Design / API6:2023 Unrestricted Access to Sensitive Business Flows
findings.impact: Weak server-side validation can enable business logic abuse.
findings.remediation: Validate value ranges server-side for every state-changing endpoint.

findings.description: An anonymously reachable index/catalog response triggered generic secret heuristics. Treat as informational until manually confirmed.
findings.owasp: A05:2025 Security Misconfiguration / API9:2023 Improper Inventory Management
reproduction: Fetch the configured catalog path(s); diff against intentional public data.
findings.impact: Pre-auth disclosure can simplify subsequent testing.
findings.remediation: Keep deployment secrets out of public metadata.

findings.type: Operational-looking strings mined from authenticated JSON
findings.description: Heuristics surfaced strings resembling codes/secrets (http://172.23.112.1:8080/api/challenges → APP02, APP05, APP06, APP07, APP08).
findings.owasp: API3:2023 Broken Object Property Level Authorization / A05:2025 Security Misconfiguration
findings.evidence: http://172.23.112.1:8080/api/challenges → APP02, APP05, APP06, APP07, APP08
reproduction: Diff JSON against the documented public schema.
findings.impact: Leakage may enable chaining by any logged-in caller.
findings.remediation: Filter fields at serialization time.

findings.description: Varying a numeric segment seen in crawler output produced HTTP 200 with token-shaped content for the harness account.
findings.owasp: A01:2025 Broken Access Control / API1:2023 Broken Object Level Authorization
reproduction: Enumerate adjacent numeric identifiers with the harness account.
findings.impact: Unauthorized object access cannot be ruled out without server-side authorization review.
findings.remediation: Authorize per resource key; deny by default.

findings.description: Optional alg=none JWT probe at api/auth/jwt-flag returned HTTP 200 with sensitive content (matrix entry {'id': 1, 'username': 'admin', 'role': 'admin', 'iat': 1777990771}). Requires --experimental-auth + operator config.
findings.owasp: A07:2025 Identification and Authentication Failures / API2:2023 Broken Authentication
reproduction: Replay payload + path triple recorded in evidence using your JWT tool of choice.
findings.impact: Trust in unsigned bearer claims breaks authentication.
findings.remediation: Reject ``alg=none`` tokens; pin verification algorithms.

findings.description: A public directory or file path exposed downloadable sensitive-looking artifacts.
findings.owasp: A05:2025 Security Misconfiguration / API3:2023 Broken Object Property Level Authorization
findings.impact: Exposed artifacts can leak source, dependencies, secrets, database backups, or internal documents.
findings.remediation: Remove backup/data files from the web root and disable directory listing.

findings.description: A crafted query parameter produced database-shaped error behavior.
reproduction: Observe database error wording or HTTP 5xx behavior.
findings.impact: The parameter may be injectable or reveal database internals useful for follow-up tooling.
findings.remediation: Use parameterized queries and generic error responses.

findings.type: JWT Algorithm None Bypass: Admin Access Confirmed
findings.description: An unsigned JWT with an elevated role claim was accepted; the endpoint returned sensitive content.
findings.owasp: A07:2025 Identification and Authentication Failures / API2:2023 Broken Authentication
reproduction: Craft a JWT with header {alg:none} and an admin role in the payload.
findings.impact: Unsigned tokens can bypass authentication and authorization checks for any caller.
findings.remediation: Reject alg=none tokens and only accept explicitly configured signed algorithms.

findings.description: Parameter `q` is vulnerable. Technique: stacked queries. Title: PostgreSQL > 8.1 stacked queries (comment).
findings.evidence: parameter=q; technique=stacked queries; title=PostgreSQL > 8.1 stacked queries (comment)
findings.remediation: Use parameterized queries and server-side allowlists for dynamic clauses.

findings.description: Parameter `q` is vulnerable. Technique: stacked queries. Title: PostgreSQL > 8.1 stacked queries (comment).
findings.evidence: parameter=q; technique=stacked queries; title=PostgreSQL > 8.1 stacked queries (comment)
findings.remediation: Use parameterized queries and server-side allowlists for dynamic clauses.
